prompt:
Marina office here. Our little slip-printing script is having a bad week, two ways.

First: since yesterday's cleanup of the letter templates it won't even load. Ruby says:

  > 18 |     letter = <<LETTER
       |                ^~~~~~ unterminated heredoc; can't find string "LETTER" anywhere before EOF
  ...
  > 37 | end
       |    ^ unexpected end-of-input, assuming it is closing the parent top level context

Which is nonsense to me because the word LETTER is plainly right there at the end of the letter, I can see it in the editor.

Second: even before it stopped loading, the checkout receipts had been coming out of the printer literally saying things like `Card ending #{rental.card_tail} charged $#{format(...)}` — the template holes printed as-is instead of being filled in. A renter taped one to the office door as a joke. So when you get it parsing again, don't be surprised if the receipts are still wrong — that one needs fixing too.

Both documents must come out filled in and flush against the left margin (the printer chokes on leading spaces). test_boat_slip.rb pins the exact expected output — make `ruby test_boat_slip.rb` green and leave the test file alone.
